title: malvastrum cavanillesii
intro: Malvastrum cavanillesii, also known as the Mexican hollyhock or Malvastrum, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Mexican medicine. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Malvastrum cavanillesii can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a versatile herb that can be easily incorporated into herbal remedies.
